notes: First ~2 weeks: faculty-support surge — keep quick-turnaround room

notes: Faculty mostly away — filming and reviews pause
Good window for editing, course build and QA backlog

Prime window to start Fall-2027 course builds

sprints.blurb: Digital Learning Sprints fund faculty — a $3,000 stipend plus ODL/TLT team support — for 1–3-month explorations (AI, flipped classrooms, COIL, async modules, VR). Annual cycle: applications due early April (2026: Apr 6), one-day accelerator ≈May 1, builds run over the summer — the cohort pre-books part of ODL's summer capacity every year.

plan_model.note: Archetype hour ranges are the estimator's calibrated ODL-staff production hours per project type (full lifecycle). Faculty time is not included — the estimator has zero faculty hours logged.

method.planned: Planned hours (Capacity Allocations) — the PRIMARY budget. For each person-month row on the Capacity sheet, that month's Allocated Hours are split EVENLY across the projects listed in that row, then summed per project across every month of the sheet. The even split is an assumption: the sheet doesn't record how a person's hours actually divide across their several projects that month.
method.task_est: Task-level estimate (Asana) — a SECONDARY reference where present: the sum of each project's tasks' 'Estimated time' in Asana.
method.actual: Actual = logged timesheet hours only (Asana time entries). Asana time entry lapsed 2026-06-24 and has just been restored, so actuals are sparse right now — read low numbers as under-logged, not as under-work.
method.consequences_over: Over ~120% of plan → the work is crowding out a person's other projects, hiding overtime, or pushing the next project's start later.
method.consequences_under: Under ~60% of plan with real activity → the plan was over-scoped, or (most likely right now) time is simply under-logged.

rules: Asana weekly status marked At risk / Off track / On hold.
The latest weekly update's text mentions a risk phrase — delay, blocker, waiting-on, scope creep, over-hours, short-staffed, or a concern — and the phrase isn't negated ("no blockers" doesn't count).
An active project with no weekly status update in more than 21 days (weekly cadence expected).
Past its Asana due date and not marked Complete.
Actual logged hours exceed ~120% of the planned (Capacity-sheet) hours.

brief_inputs._readme: Manual weekly inputs for the Director Brief tab. Edit this file (or via the pipeline) and re-run build.py. The brief's other sections (Top 5, Projects needing attention, Phase congestion, YTD stats, Estimate vs actual, Recommendations progress, derived wins/round-up candidates) are AUTO-DERIVED from the workbook + Asana snapshot and do NOT live here.

ops._note: Set timesheet_compliance_pct to the team's current timesheet-submission rate (0-100). We don't pull this automatically yet, so it's a manual number for now. Leave null to show 'not tracked yet'.

intake.name: I CAN HELP Module – Faculty
intake.description: We would like to take the basic structure of the ICH module we just created with ODL for students and adjust it into a faculty module. We would also like to explore the best platform for hosting the content. We partner with University Counseling Center on this project and we would like to host the module in the same place they host the faculty toolkit (Kaneb Center site).

intake.name: Digital Learning Suite for Talk With Your Baby – Robinson Community Learning Center
intake.description: I am tasked with the following grant project outcome: the creation of a suite of digital resources utilized by both parents and early care providers for further education and to support the acquisition of core capacities that promote brain and language development in babies and young children. Performance Indicators: through a well-trafficked website serving as a hub of information in Indiana, both parents and providers will be able to access user-friendly, high-quality learning tools, as evidenced by the creation of: 12 "how-to" videos modeling core capacities in action; a repository of research briefs and user-friendly fact sheets; a texting mechanism for pushing out research-based tips; and an open-access unit of study for institutions preparing future teachers and providers. I would like a consultation about how to get started in the creation of a website, and recommendations for software and organization of this project. Perhaps this becomes a partnership?

Take the latest meeting's Shoutouts + Project Updates. Refresh weekly. This feeds 'Wins & momentum' AND the 'Round-up draft' tab.

reflection_considerations._meta: Key considerations for future projects, pulled verbatim from each ODL reflection report in the 'Project Reflection Reports 2025' Drive folder. Refreshable by re-running this extraction.

reports.report_title: 3D Environments Reflection Report
key_considerations: During the intake process, we need to do a better job of balancing what the PI needs/wants and what we know are best practices and come to an understanding on the workflow that reflects best practices and not an unproductive compromise.

When a PM sees red lights flashing, they should not just try to resolve or smooth them over in the moment but let the ODL leadership team know to preempt smoke from turning into a fire. We need to both listen and hear then address the PI’s concerns fully for the long term.

reports.report_title: ACE Ascent Phase IV Evaluation Report
key_considerations: The beginning stages of the project felt somewhat rushed for the ODL team. The issue, again, seems to be related to travel with pre-set travel dates causing a sense of urgency.
Different or refined SOPs for travel that reflect the particular circumstances of the project. The same travel SOPs don’t make sense for a trip to Africa and a trip to Kansas.
When hiring/engaging someone for voiceover work, send a sample of the voiceover to the various stakeholders to be sure everyone agrees that we have the right voice before committing to that person.

reports.project: AI Critical Thinking Tutor Summer Sprint
reports.report_title: Critical Thinking AI Tutor Summer Sprint Reflection Report
key_considerations: The Sprints should be split into AI and non-AI groups. Maybe all of the awardees could be together for one early activity and then get the two groups in separate spaces with the resources (e.g. AI specialists) they need.
Without an in-house AI specialist, we should rethink our role for AI-based Sprints as a convener and facilitator rather than as the owner of the project.
We should determine if we are going to develop our own AI expertise or focus on being the convener/connector and lean into one of those models. We do not have the current AI credibility.
We should take advantage of Paul’s interest in publishing his data to continue to partner with him in that process so that we can demonstrate impact.

reports.report_title: Retrospective Reflection Report: AI for the ND Community
key_considerations: (AI Transparency Statement) We need to prevent situations in which SMEs use AI in inappropriate ways (such as not checking information) or without our knowledge.
Having too many big projects at once creates risks, including work that is rushed and pressure on the team.
Hold ourselves accountable to the timelines just as we would SME/partners

reports.report_title: Evaluation Report_ AI Video Andrea Russo
Evaluation Report: AI Video Nathaniel Myers
key_considerations: Although Prof. Myers did not get all of the desired deliverables (due to time constraints on his end), he still found the conversations and learning design process (listing objectives, the class-by-class listing of assets in the course map) valuable. This might imply that the ODL could provide benefit at scale through a workshop(s) in which participants would learn the basics of developing a course map and then follow through on that on their own.

reports.report_title: ASCEND Engineering Reflection Report
key_considerations: We should be creating a full-model structure for an ASCEND implementation, starting with an overall strategy for identifying areas for application and partners. Then those partners need to be onboarded effectively with the constraints and timelines highlighted. We should also have a workflow for redesigning the base ASCEND content–making clear what exists, what needs adaptation, and what needs to be created anew. Finally, we need a mechanism for assessing our effectiveness that surfaces insights from the students and coaches.

reports.report_title: Retrospective Reflection Report: Bringing Korean to Life.docx
key_considerations: Proper documentation and support for future interns
Continue pairing Canvas modules/courses with XR experiences for accessibility and educational enrichment

reports.report_title: Calc I Summer Online Reflection Report
key_considerations: Start the process/project sooner to avoid rush
Need a financial guarantee for partners whose course might get cancelled
Start Summer Online course projects in the fall if possible.
Each project should come with estimates of the time required of SME as part of the onboarding process or charter.

reports.report_title: Evaluation Report: Carbon Neutral
key_considerations: It is important for ODL to have a relatively clear understanding of the time commitment required of a faculty member prior to the project’s formal acceptance and to make that time commitment clear to the partnering faculty member. It might be helpful to have some type of milestones/timeline image to mark the progress of a project and the remaining time constraints and requirements.

It is important for ODL to make the phases of the project clear and distinct to the partnering faculty member. Again, a timeline image with milestones that also delineates the phases might be helpful.

reports.report_title: Evaluation Report Conveying Visual Data
key_considerations: Line in the project charter going forward to allow ODL to pause or stop the project at our discretion
Provide 1st-time faculty members guidance with any filming to enable them to be comfortable with the process
Track the amount of time faculty take to better set expectations at discovery phase
Need to safeguard the team when faculty cannot complete the project as promised

reports.report_title: Force for Good Video Project Reflection Report
key_considerations: A more specific and direct partnership with FTT might be valuable on several levels.
Putting time markers on agenda items can help with time management.
The PM needs to ensure that faculty partners have a clear understanding of the purpose for each meeting and their responsibilities for preparing and participating.
The PM should feel empowered to cut off unproductive meeting conversations.

reports.report_title: Retrospective Reflection Report: Global Health VR.docx
key_considerations: Proper documentation and support for future interns
Continue pairing Canvas modules/courses with XR experiences for accessibility and educational enrichment

reports.report_title: Retrospective Summary Report_HPL.docx
key_considerations: For projects similar to HPL - we should consider putting more time into upfront planning and design. The team was in agreement that the project needed a couple more months of time to be fully executed
Citations, accessibility knowledge, and engaging with the OGC should be introduced early in the process
Setting work expectations for the team would help, especially with faculty changing their minds frequently

reports.report_title: Retrospective Summary Report_Lab Prep.docx
key_considerations: Prioritize rehearsal time for faculty to strengthen delivery and reduce editing complexity.
Introduce a mid-point alignment session early in the sprint to confirm scope and expectations.
Balance faculty creativity with project scope - keep videos concise but allow space for innovative filming approaches.
Leverage external expertise strategically (as with LabFlow) to keep ODL’s focus on core design and media strengths.

reports.report_title: Retrospective Summary Report_MS ACMS_with link.docx
key_considerations: Share Zoom recording guidelines and technical expectations earlier in the process.
Recommend or provide basic recording equipment for remote faculty when feasible.
Ensure consistent ODL oversight for all interviews to maintain visual and audio standards.
Continue leveraging informal, low-script production models for promotional or awareness-focused projects, where appropriate.

reports.report_title: Retrospective Summary Report_MSAN II with links.docx
key_considerations: Ensure proper time is in place for QA to ensure smooth transition
Engage with TLT for badges and other technology earlier in the process

reports.report_title: Untitled document
Evaluation Report PIE Spiritual Reflection Erika Bramley
Evaluation Report: PIE Video #1 Tim O’Malley
The Qur’an and the Bible Reflection Report
key_considerations: Create a sandbox version of the course. Give the SME a tour and let them play around and get comfortable with the technology and things like the Dialogue tool.
Use data in advance to set realistic expectations for the SME in terms of the expected number of students and revenue.

reports.report_title: R&E AI Reflection Report
key_considerations: Develop a more robust onboarding and management process for Teaching Fellows.
Leverage Valya’s laudatory feedback.
Have informed limits to the number of assets we agree to produce.
To a better job of setting up an evaluation/data gathering process from the start.
Clarify roles, responsibilities, and division of labor better with freelancers.

reports.report_title: Evaluation Report - Sprint: Parent Empowerment
Retrospective Summary Report_Stat Flipped Classroom.docx
key_considerations: Extend timelines for large flipped-classroom projects to match the scope.
Stagger project sprints across ODL to avoid overlapping media and design workloads.
Lock the scope early to prevent late additions of videos or features.
Deliver videos for review in smaller batches to avoid overwhelming faculty.
Dedicate onboarding time early in the project to set clear expectations and reduce confusion later.

reports.report_title: Retrospective Summary Report_Study Abroad.docx
key_considerations: Clarify the scope early regarding media expectations and deliverables.
Stagger onboarding so contributors join with full context.
Keep usability central when working on communication/resource-style projects.
Maintain visible progress updates (weekly check-ins proved valuable here).
Leverage intern leadership strategically, balancing autonomy with structured support.

reports.report_title: Transforming FIN/MSBR Summer Sprint Reflection Report
key_considerations: There could have been cross-team collaboration between ODL and TLT and AI Lab. We should invite Alex to be part of anything AI related.
Build a project evaluation process into the Sprints. Make it a required part of the Sprints to help us track some of the student-centered outcomes.
Maybe have separate tracks for AI and non-AI sprints.

reports.report_title: Evaluation Report: Ugandan Immersion
key_considerations: Don’t know if Prof. Gallagher’s team was shown any examples of the types of work ODL could produce, but from the survey response, it seems as if that might have helped the project and what ODL was capable of doing to be less “abstract.” A best practice might be to show partners final products of similar projects.

It does not appear as if there was a charter for this project where any questions about budgeting and expenses could have been made clear. There should probably be a charter with clear budget and expense statements for any project beyond a simple, one-off consultation.

ODL partners generally seem incredibly impressed with our team as people and professionals, as well as the work we produce. We need to learn how to showcase all of that in advance–both to attract work and to instill confidence in partners for whom the design process might be a bit abstract.

reports.project: Writing in the Age of AI
reports.report_title: Evaluation Report: Writing in the Age of AI
key_considerations: In general, Prof. Myers seemed most engaged by and satisfied with the phases of the process that are more action and reflection/review focused–that is, he prefers the give and take and the learning involved from engagement more so than the set up and explaining parts of the process. This is not an uncommon theme in the feedback provided by faculty partners. Perhaps there are ways for ODL to streamline or revamp our the parts of our intake, design, and pre-production processes in which we are soliciting insight and/or preparing the faculty for their more active parts in the process.

themes.theme: Start earlier, protect the timeline, lock scope
themes.what_it_means: The most common lesson by far: projects felt rushed, needed more up-front planning, and scope crept late. Begin sooner, lock scope early, stage/stagger the work so timelines match the real effort, and cap how many big projects run at once.

themes.theme: Onboard faculty & make the process concrete
themes.what_it_means: Faculty engage best when the process is tangible: show examples of similar finished work, give them a sandbox to try the tech, share recording/technical guidelines early, and streamline the intake/design steps where we prepare them for their active role.

reports.project: Writing in the Age of AI

themes.theme: Set faculty expectations up front — charter, time commitment, phases
themes.what_it_means: Make the faculty partner's time commitment, the project phases, budget, and deliverables explicit in a charter BEFORE formally accepting a project, and track how much faculty time it actually takes so future estimates get sharper.

themes.theme: Decide ODL's AI role; collaborate across teams
themes.what_it_means: For AI-heavy work, decide whether ODL is the builder or the convener/facilitator (we currently lack in-house AI credibility), split AI vs non-AI sprint tracks, and pull partners in early — TLT, the AI Lab, external vendors — instead of owning everything.

reports.project: AI Critical Thinking Tutor Summer Sprint

themes.theme: Build evaluation & impact data in from the start
themes.what_it_means: Make outcome evaluation a required, up-front part of each project (especially Sprints) so we surface student/coach insights and can demonstrate impact — rather than bolting it on at the end.

reports.project: AI Critical Thinking Tutor Summer Sprint

themes.theme: Protect the team when faculty can't deliver
themes.what_it_means: Several projects stalled on the faculty side. Build safeguards — a charter clause that lets ODL pause or stop, a financial guarantee for at-risk courses, and PM authority to keep scope and meetings on track — so the team isn't left exposed.
Protect QA time, review deliverables in smaller batches, keep consistent ODL oversight of filming/interviews, and rehearse with faculty to reduce editing load and smooth transitions.
Leave proper documentation and support for future interns, and give interns structured leadership opportunities that balance autonomy with support.

Empower PMs to run tight meetings (clear purpose, time markers, cutting off unproductive tangents) and to escalate red flags to ODL leadership early — 'preempt smoke from turning into a fire' — rather than smoothing them over in the moment.
